values.name: checkbox
values.comment:
Makes a checkbox field.

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: checkboxDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = False
, tipe = FieldType.Checkbox
}`

values.name: date
values.comment:
Makes a date input field. By default, dates 100 years from the start of the Unix epoch (00:00:00 UTC on 1 January 1970) are allowed. Use setters to customise this.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setDateOfBirth`
  - `setDateFuture`
  - `setDatePast`
  - `setDateBounds Time.Posix Time.Posix`
  - `setDateOffset ( TimeExtra.Interval, Int ) ( TimeExtra.Interval, Int )`

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: dateDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, tipe = { min = Nothing, max = Nothing }
, value = ""
, parsedDate = Nothing
}`

values.name: email
values.comment:
Makes an email input field.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setForbiddenEmailDomains (List EmailFormat.ForbiddenDomain)`

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: emailDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, regexValidation = []
, tipe = FieldType.Email
, value = ""
}`

values.name: httpSelect
values.comment:
Makes a remotely fetched drop-down select field.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setDefault String`
  - `setOptions (RemoteData.RemoteData (HttpDetailed.Error String) (List Option.Option))`
  - `setPlaceholder String`
  - `setRemoteUrl String`
  - `setSelectablePlaceholder`

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: httpSelectDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = ""
, url = ""
, default = Nothing
, options = RemoteData.NotAsked
, placeholder = ""
, hasSelectablePlaceholder = False
}`

values.name: integer
values.comment:
Makes an age input field.

Common builders:

  - `setWidth Width.Width`

  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: integerDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No

, tipe = { min = Nothing, max = Nothing }
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = Nothing
}`

values.comment: Keep existing field if the value is Nothing

values.comment:
Makes a remotely fetched multi select field.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setPlaceholder String`
  - `setRemoteUrl String`

  - `setLabel String`

  - `setOrder Int`

  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = Set.empty
, options = RemoteData.NotAsked
, placeholder = ""
, showDropdown = False
, url = ""
}`

values.name: multiSelect
values.comment:
Makes a multi select field from a list of options.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setOptions (List Option.Option)`
  - `setPlaceholder String`

A list of options needs to be passed in, but setters passed in will override these.

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: multiSelectDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = Set.empty
, options = []
, placeholder = ""
, showDropdown = False
}`

values.name: radio
values.comment:
Makes a radio field.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setDefault String`
  - `setOptions (List Option.Option)`
  - `setDirection Direction.Direction`

A list of options needs to be passed in, but setters passed in will override these.

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: radioEnum
values.comment:
Makes a radio field for selection of Yes/No/N/A enums, with all three as an option by default. Override with setOptions.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setDefault RadioEnum.Value`
  - `setOptions (List RadioEnum.Value)`

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: radioEnumDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = Nothing
, default = Nothing
, options = [ RadioEnum.Yes, RadioEnum.No, RadioEnum.NA ]
}`

values.name: searchableMultiSelect
values.comment:
Makes a searchable multi select field, with two categories of options: searchable and non-searchable. Neither category is mandatory, so make sure to set at least one for the field to be useful.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setOptions (List Option.Option)`
  - `setSearchableOptions (List Option.Option)`
  - `setPlaceholder String`

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`
  - `setEnabledBy String`
  - `setOrder Int`
  - `setDisabled`
  - `setHidden`
  - `setUnhiddenBy String`

values.name: searchableMultiSelectDefault
values.comment:
`{ required = Required.No
, label = ""
, width = Width.FullSize
, enabledBy = Nothing
, order = 0
, disabled = False
, hidden = False
, unhiddenBy = Nothing
, value = Set.empty
, options = []
, placeholder = ""
, showDropdown = False
, searchableOptions = []
, searchInput = ""
}`

values.name: searchableSelect
values.comment:
Makes a searchable select field, with options that are searchable.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setDefault String`
  - \`setOptions (List Option.Option)
  - `setPlaceholder String`
  - `setSelectablePlaceholder`

Common builders:

  - `setIsRequired Required.IsRequired`
  - `setLabel String`
  - `setWidth Width.Width`

values.name: select
values.comment:
Makes a drop-down select field from a list of options.

In addition to the common builders, the following are available:

  - `setOptions (List Option.Option)`
  - `setPlaceholder String`
  - `setSelectablePlaceholder`

A list of options needs to be passed in, but setters passed in will override these.

values.name: setDateFuture
values.comment: Sets the selectable date range to be the next 10 years.

values.name: setDateOfBirth
values.comment: Sets the selectable date range to be the last 120 years.

values.name: setDatePast
values.comment: Sets the selectable date range to be the last 120 years.

values.name: setDefault
values.type: d -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | default : Maybe.Maybe d } -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | default : Maybe.Maybe d }

values.name: setMax
values.comment: Sets the max integer value.

values.name: setMaxDate
values.comment: Sets the max selectable date range to specified absolute date in POSIX time.

values.name: setMaxDateOffset
values.comment:
Sets the max selectable date range to specified interval relative to the current date.
For example, to select a max date 5 years from now, use:
`setMaxDateOffset ( TimeExtra.Year, 5 )`

values.name: setMin
values.comment: Sets the min integer value.

values.name: setMinDate
values.comment: Sets the max selectable date range to specified absolute date in POSIX time.

values.name: setMinDateOffset
values.comment:
Sets the max selectable date range to specified interval relative to the current date.
For example, to select a min date of 2 years ago, use:
`setMinDateOffset ( TimeExtra.Year, 2 )`

values.name: setOptions
values.type: o -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | options : o } -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | options : o }

values.name: setPlaceholder
values.type: p -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | placeholder : p } -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | placeholder : p }

values.name: setRemoteUrl
values.type: String.String -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | url : String.String } -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | url : String.String }

values.name: setSelectablePlaceholder
values.type: Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | hasSelectablePlaceholder : Basics.Bool } -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | hasSelectablePlaceholder : Basics.Bool }

values.name: setValue
values.type: v -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | value : v } -> Form.Field.FieldProperties { a | value : v }

Constructs a Regex that forbids a string from having the provided suffixes.
Avoids lookarounds to ensure compatibility with WebKit-based browsers.

name: Form.Lib.Time

values.name: offsetDay
values.comment: Offsets a time by the given number of days and returns a new time, floored to the start of the day.

values.name: offsetYear
values.comment: Offsets a time by the given number of years and returns a new time, floored to the start of the year.

values.name: toDateString
values.comment: Uses Iso8601.fromTime produces a date with timestamp and takes first 10 characters to return "YYYY-MM-DD"
